independent_claims.preamble: A display driving circuit for driving a display panel

elements.text:
a data line, a sensing line, and a pixel connected to the data line and the sensing line, the display driving circuit comprising:
 a control logic controlling an operation of the display driving circuit

a source amplifier comprising a first input terminal, a second input terminal, and an output terminal, the source amplifier amplifying a pixel voltage for displaying an image on the display panel and providing the amplified pixel voltage to the pixel through the data line
a first switching element connected between the first input terminal and the output terminal

and 
 a second switching element connected between the first input terminal and the sensing line
the control logic performs a first control to control the first switching element and the second switching element
the source amplifier amplifies the pixel voltage, and performs a second control to control the first switching element and the second switching element

the source amplifier compares a sample voltage that is input to the first input terminal through the sensing line with a comparison voltage input to the second input terminal and outputs comparison data based on a comparison result of the sample voltage with the comparison voltage

independent_claims.full_text:
A display driving circuit for driving a display panel including a plurality of data lines, a plurality of sensing lines, and a plurality of pixels connected to the plurality of data lines and the plurality of sensing lines, the display driving circuit comprising:
 a plurality of decoders that correspond respectively to the plurality of data lines, each decoder converting pixel data of a corresponding data line and outputting a pixel voltage; 
 a plurality of source amplifiers that correspond respectively to the plurality of decoders, each source amplifier comprising a first input terminal, a second input terminal and an output terminal, and amplifying a corresponding pixel voltage and providing the amplified pixel voltage to a corresponding pixel through the corresponding data line; 
 a plurality of first switching elements that correspond respectively to the plurality of source amplifiers, each first switching element connected between the first input terminal and the output terminal of a corresponding source amplifier; and 
 a plurality of second switching elements that corresponding respectively to the plurality of source amplifiers, each second switching element connected between the first input terminal of the corresponding source amplifier and a corresponding sensing line, 
 wherein each of the plurality of source amplifiers, 
 in a first operation mode, based on a first operation of a corresponding one of the plurality of first switching elements and a corresponding one of the plurality of second switching elements, amplifies the corresponding pixel voltage to be output to the corresponding data line, and,

independent_claims.preamble: A method of fabricating a multi-functional platform

elements.text:
at least one gas sensor, the method comprising the steps of:
 providing a printed circuit board (PCB) including a chip integrated on the PCB, the chip including a substrate
providing a picoliter dispenser having a moveable dispenser head with a nozzle
forming a gas sensor base layer of the at least one gas sensor by depositing a nanostructure directly on the substrate of the chip, the nanostructure including an unfunctionalized nanomaterial

and 
 functionalizing the gas sensor base layer by depositing a functional group of nanomaterial to the gas sensor base layer using the picoliter dispenser so as to form the at least one gas sensor

the step of forming the sensor base layer includes the steps of preparing a suspension of the unfunctionalized nanomaterial of the sensor base layer and depositing the suspension of the unfunctionalized nanomaterial directly on the substrate of the chip using the picoliter dispenser so as to form the sensor base layer of the at least one gas sensor

independent_claims.preamble: A method of fabricating a multi-functional platform

elements.text:
a plurality of gas sensors, the method comprising the steps of:
 providing a printed circuit board (PCB) including a chip integrated on the PCB, the chip including a substrate
providing a picoliter dispenser having a moveable dispenser head with a nozzle
preparing a suspension of unfunctionalized nanomaterials provided for forming the plurality of gas sensors

functionalizing the suspension of the unfunctionalized nanomaterials by mixing the unfunctionalized suspension with a plurality of different functional groups so as to form a plurality of functionalized suspensions each having different functionalized nanomaterials

depositing a first of the plurality of functionalized suspensions having a first functionalized nanomaterial directly to the chip using the picoliter dispenser so as to form a first of the plurality of gas sensors

and

independent_claims.preamble: A driving support device for a vehicle, the driving support device

elements.text:
:
 a processor configured to execute non-transitory program instructions stored in a memory to: 
 calculate a sensitivity index value for avoiding a target that is at risk of collision, based on a driving operation content of a driver of the vehicle

determine a driving support method to be applied to the vehicle as support that is provided to control the distance in the lateral direction between the vehicle and the target when the vehicle passes the target, in a case where the sensitivity index value calculated by the processor is higher than a predetermined threshold, or as support that is provided to control the speed of the vehicle when the vehicle passes by the target in a case where the sensitivity index value is lower than the predetermined threshold

and 
 execute the support to control the distance in the lateral direction or the support to control the speed of the vehicle determined by the processor

the processor calculates the sensitivity index value of the driver based on a distance between the vehicle and the target in a front-rear direction, a sense of the driver regarding the distance in the front-rear direction, a distance between the vehicle and the target in a lateral direction, a sense of the driver regarding the distance in the lateral direction, a speed of the vehicle, a speed of the target, and a sense of the driver regarding the speed

independent_claims.full_text:
A display device, comprising:
 a substrate in which a plurality of first pixel areas and a plurality of second pixel areas each including a plurality of sub pixels are defined; 
 a plurality of pixel circuits which is disposed in each of the plurality of sub pixels on the substrate and includes a driving transistor; 
 a plurality of reflection plates which is disposed in each of the plurality of sub pixels on the plurality of pixel circuits and is electrically connected to the driving transistor of each of the plurality of pixel circuits; 
 a plurality of welding transistors which is disposed between the plurality of first pixel areas and the plurality of second pixel areas and selectively connects the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas; and 
 a plurality of light emitting diodes disposed on the plurality of reflection plates in at least some sub pixels of the plurality of sub pixels, 
 wherein each of the plurality of light emitting diodes is electrically connected to the driving transistor through each of the plurality of reflection plates, 
 wherein the plurality of sub pixels includes a first sub pixel, a second sub pixel, a third sub pixel, and a fourth sub pixel, 
 the plurality of reflection plates includes a first reflection plate disposed in the first sub pixel, a second reflection plate disposed in the second sub pixel, a third reflection plate disposed in the third sub pixel, and a fourth reflection plate disposed in the fourth sub pixel, and 
 each of the plurality of welding transistors connects the first reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the first reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, the second reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the second reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, the third reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the third reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, and the fourth reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the fourth reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, respectively, 
 wherein the plurality of light emitting diodes are disposed in the plurality of first pixel areas between the plurality of first pixel areas and the plurality of second pixel areas, 
 in a low gray scale mode, the welding transistor is turned off and electrically separates each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, and 
 in a high gray scale mode, the welding transistor is turned on and electrically connects each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas.

 a substrate in which a plurality of first pixel areas and a plurality of second pixel areas each including a plurality of sub pixels are defined
a plurality of pixel circuits which is disposed in each of the plurality of sub pixels on the substrate and includes a driving transistor

a plurality of reflection plates which is disposed in each of the plurality of sub pixels on the plurality of pixel circuits and is electrically connected to the driving transistor of each of the plurality of pixel circuits

a plurality of welding transistors which is disposed between the plurality of first pixel areas and the plurality of second pixel areas and selectively connects the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas

and 
 a plurality of light emitting diodes disposed on the plurality of reflection plates in at least some sub pixels of the plurality of sub pixels
each of the plurality of light emitting diodes is electrically connected to the driving transistor through each of the plurality of reflection plates

the plurality of sub pixels includes a first sub pixel, a second sub pixel, a third sub pixel, and a fourth sub pixel, 
 the plurality of reflection plates includes a first reflection plate disposed in the first sub pixel, a second reflection plate disposed in the second sub pixel, a third reflection plate disposed in the third sub pixel, and a fourth reflection plate disposed in the fourth sub pixel, and 
 each of the plurality of welding transistors connects the first reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the first reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, the second reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the second reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, the third reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the third reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, and the fourth reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the fourth reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, respectively

the plurality of light emitting diodes are disposed in the plurality of first pixel areas between the plurality of first pixel areas and the plurality of second pixel areas, 
 in a low gray scale mode, the welding transistor is turned off and electrically separates each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, and 
 in a high gray scale mode, the welding transistor is turned on and electrically connects each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas

:
 collecting, by a processing system including at least one processor, orientation data from a mobile device as a user of the mobile device moves within an indoor space
generating, by the processing system, orientation estimates for the mobile device from the orientation data

generating, by the processing system, distance estimates representing distances of the mobile device to a wireless access point in the indoor space, simultaneously with the generating the orientation estimates
storing, by the processing system, the orientation estimates and the distance estimates together as a series of data blocks
constructing, by the processing system, a plurality of trajectory segments from the orientation estimates

identifying, by the processing system, identifiers for a last number of trajectory segments of the plurality of trajectory segments, using a subset of the series of data blocks corresponding to the last number of trajectory segments

the identifying the identifiers comprises inputting data blocks of the series of data blocks corresponding to the last number of trajectory segments to a classifier that is trained to output the identifiers based on the data blocks that are inputted

and 
 identifying, by the processing system, a predicted place within the indoor space to which the user is expected to go by using the identifiers to traverse a prediction tree, where branches of the prediction tree that are associated with the identifiers define a path that ends at a leaf node of the prediction tree that corresponds to the predicted place

independent_claims.full_text:
A reinforcement board for flexible printed circuit board, comprising a substrate, wherein the substrate comprises a first surface and a second surface which are opposite to each other, the substrate is provided with a through hole penetrating through the first surface and the second surface, wherein each of the first surface, the second surface and a hole wall of the through hole is provided with a conductive layer, the conductive layers on the first surface and the second surface are communicated with each other by the conductive layer on the hole wall of the through hole, and the second surface is a connecting surface facing the flexible printed circuit board when the reinforcement board is fixed on the flexible printed circuit board; and
 the conductive layer on the second surface is grid-shaped, the conductive layer on the second surface comprises a plurality of wires crossing each other, the plurality of wires crossing each other comprise a plurality of wires extending in a transverse direction and a plurality of wires extending in a longitudinal direction, grid holes are formed between the plurality of wires extending in the transverse direction and the plurality of wires extending in the longitudinal direction, and the grid holes are a plurality of openings which expose or do not expose the second surface.

independent_claims.preamble: A system for identification of command injection attacks, the system

elements.text:
:
 one or more devices connected to a network
a non-transitory memory device, said non-transitory memory device having modules of instruction code stored thereon
an execution platform executing executable products, said execution platform is connected to said one or more devices via said network
said one or more devices are configured to provide execution commands to said execution platform
one or more processors associated with said memory device

at least one processor of said one or more processors is configured to execute the modules of instruction code, whereupon execution of said modules of instruction code said at least one processor is further configured to: 
 analyze execution commands provided to said execution platform to identify input provisioning events or execution events
in response to identifying a potential input provisioning event, determine that an input of the potential input provisioning event includes a command separator and an executable product
the input of the potential input provisioning event is received from said network
the command separator is a character or combination of characters configured to separate between two commands
in response to said determining that the input includes the command separator and the executable product, record a suspicious record event
in response to identifying a potential execution event, determine that an execution command of the potential execution event correlates to the suspicious record event
the execution command is configured to be executed by the potential execution event
in response to said determining that the execution command correlates to the suspicious record event, flag the execution command as a command injection attack

and 
 perform a remedial action with respect to the flagged command injection attack prior to attempting to execute the execution command by said execution platform

a non-transitory computer readable storage medium retaining program instructions, which program instructions when read by a processor, cause the processor to perform:
 analyzing execution commands provided to an execution platform, the execution platform is executing executable products, the execution platform is connected to one or more devices via a network
the one or more devices are configured to provide execution commands to the execution platform
the execution platform is configured to identify input provisioning events or execution events

and

independent_claims.full_text:
A text recognition apparatus, comprising:
 a memory configured to store a control program for text recognition; and 
 a processor configured to execute the control program stored in the memory, 
 wherein the processor is configured to collect RGB images, extract hand joint information from the RGB images, generate a text image based on the hand joint information, recognize text from the text image, and output the recognized text, wherein the processor is configured to preprocess the text image, and infer the text by inputting the preprocessed text image to a first machine-learning model, wherein the processor is configured to convert the text image into a grayscale image, adjust a resolution of the grayscale image, normalize the image of which the resolution is adjusted so that pixel values of the image are 0 to 1, and convert the normalized image into a tensor.

elements.text:
:
 a memory configured to store a control program for text recognition

and 
 a processor configured to execute the control program stored in the memory

the processor is configured to collect RGB images, extract hand joint information from the RGB images, generate a text image based on the hand joint information, recognize text from the text image, and output the recognized text
the processor is configured to preprocess the text image, and infer the text by inputting the preprocessed text image to a first machine-learning model

the processor is configured to convert the text image into a grayscale image, adjust a resolution of the grayscale image, normalize the image of which the resolution is adjusted so that pixel values of the image are 0 to 1, and convert the normalized image into a tensor

element_refs.full_claim_text:
A display driving circuit for driving a display panel including a plurality of data lines, a plurality of sensing lines, and a plurality of pixels connected to the plurality of data lines and the plurality of sensing lines, the display driving circuit comprising:
 a plurality of decoders that correspond respectively to the plurality of data lines, each decoder converting pixel data of a corresponding data line and outputting a pixel voltage; 
 a plurality of source amplifiers that correspond respectively to the plurality of decoders, each source amplifier comprising a first input terminal, a second input terminal and an output terminal, and amplifying a corresponding pixel voltage and providing the amplified pixel voltage to a corresponding pixel through the corresponding data line; 
 a plurality of first switching elements that correspond respectively to the plurality of source amplifiers, each first switching element connected between the first input terminal and the output terminal of a corresponding source amplifier; and 
 a plurality of second switching elements that corresponding respectively to the plurality of source amplifiers, each second switching element connected between the first input terminal of the corresponding source amplifier and a corresponding sensing line, 
 wherein each of the plurality of source amplifiers, 
 in a first operation mode, based on a first operation of a corresponding one of the plurality of first switching elements and a corresponding one of the plurality of second switching elements, amplifies the corresponding pixel voltage to be output to the corresponding data line, and, 
 in a second operation mode, based on a second operation of the corresponding one of the plurality of first switching elements and the corresponding one of the plurality of second switching elements, compares a sample voltage input to the first input terminal through the corresponding sensing line with a comparison voltage input to the second input terminal and outputs comparison data.

 terminal connectors, each (i) extending through one of the terminal receiving orifices, (ii) connected to one of the bus bars, and (iii) protruding outward from the second outer surface of the insulating material.

A display device, comprising:

 a pixel circuit which is disposed in each of the plurality of sub pixels, the pixel circuit including a driving transistor; and

 wherein the welding transistor is disposed between the pixel circuit of the first pixel area and the pixel circuit of the second pixel area, and selectively controls the display panel to operate in a high resolution mode or a low resolution mode.

A display device, comprising:
 a substrate in which a plurality of first pixel areas and a plurality of second pixel areas each including a plurality of sub pixels are defined; 
 a plurality of pixel circuits which is disposed in each of the plurality of sub pixels on the substrate and includes a driving transistor; 
 a plurality of reflection plates which is disposed in each of the plurality of sub pixels on the plurality of pixel circuits and is electrically connected to the driving transistor of each of the plurality of pixel circuits; 
 a plurality of welding transistors which is disposed between the plurality of first pixel areas and the plurality of second pixel areas and selectively connects the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas; and 
 a plurality of light emitting diodes disposed on the plurality of reflection plates in at least some sub pixels of the plurality of sub pixels, 
 wherein each of the plurality of light emitting diodes is electrically connected to the driving transistor through each of the plurality of reflection plates, 
 wherein the plurality of sub pixels includes a first sub pixel, a second sub pixel, a third sub pixel, and a fourth sub pixel, 
 the plurality of reflection plates includes a first reflection plate disposed in the first sub pixel, a second reflection plate disposed in the second sub pixel, a third reflection plate disposed in the third sub pixel, and a fourth reflection plate disposed in the fourth sub pixel, and 
 each of the plurality of welding transistors connects the first reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the first reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, the second reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the second reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, the third reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the third reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, and the fourth reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and the fourth reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, respectively, 
 wherein the plurality of light emitting diodes are disposed in the plurality of first pixel areas between the plurality of first pixel areas and the plurality of second pixel areas, 
 in a low gray scale mode, the welding transistor is turned off and electrically separates each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas, and 
 in a high gray scale mode, the welding transistor is turned on and electrically connects each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of first pixel areas and each of the plurality of reflection plates of the plurality of second pixel areas.

 an external coupling wire coupled to at least one of the first and the second terminals; and 
 a conductive member that is provided at an end part of the panel unit in a second direction intersecting the first direction and extends in the first direction, 
 wherein 
 the conductive member
 extends from a light adjustment panel positioned closest to one side in the first direction in the panel unit to a light adjustment panel positioned closest to another side, and 
 couples the first terminal on the first substrate and the second terminal on the second substrate in each of the light adjustment panels, 
 
 in the light adjustment panel provided in the panel unit and positioned closest to the one side in the first direction, 
 the second substrate is disposed on the one side of the first substrate in the first direction, 
 a translucent conductive film coupled to the conductive member is formed on a surface of the second substrate on the one side in the first direction, and 
 the translucent conductive film is coupled to the external coupling wire.

A reinforcement board for flexible printed circuit board, comprising a substrate, wherein the substrate comprises a first surface and a second surface which are opposite to each other, the substrate is provided with a through hole penetrating through the first surface and the second surface, wherein each of the first surface, the second surface and a hole wall of the through hole is provided with a conductive layer, the conductive layers on the first surface and the second surface are communicated with each other by the conductive layer on the hole wall of the through hole, and the second surface is a connecting surface facing the flexible printed circuit board when the reinforcement board is fixed on the flexible printed circuit board; and
 the conductive layer on the second surface is grid-shaped, the conductive layer on the second surface comprises a plurality of wires crossing each other, the plurality of wires crossing each other comprise a plurality of wires extending in a transverse direction and a plurality of wires extending in a longitudinal direction, grid holes are formed between the plurality of wires extending in the transverse direction and the plurality of wires extending in the longitudinal direction, and the grid holes are a plurality of openings which expose or do not expose the second surface.

A text recognition apparatus, comprising:
 a memory configured to store a control program for text recognition; and 
 a processor configured to execute the control program stored in the memory, 
 wherein the processor is configured to collect RGB images, extract hand joint information from the RGB images, generate a text image based on the hand joint information, recognize text from the text image, and output the recognized text, wherein the processor is configured to preprocess the text image, and infer the text by inputting the preprocessed text image to a first machine-learning model, wherein the processor is configured to convert the text image into a grayscale image, adjust a resolution of the grayscale image, normalize the image of which the resolution is adjusted so that pixel values of the image are 0 to 1, and convert the normalized image into a tensor.

comparison_matrix.feature_description: An electronic circuit responsible for regulating the power flow from the solar panel to the integrated battery pack and to the USB-C output, preventing overcharge or deep discharge.
comparison_matrix.feature_patent_language: An electronic circuit configured to regulate power flow from the solar panel to the battery pack and USB-C output, preventing overcharge or deep discharge.
comparison_matrix.element_text: the solar charging layer electrically connects the light source and is configured to charge the light source
comparison_matrix.gemini_explanation: This element requires a component identified as a 'solar charging layer' that establishes an electrical connection with a 'light source,' and whose functionality includes providing power to the light source to effectuate charging.
comparison_matrix.gemini_assessment: Both the feature and the claim element relate to utilizing solar energy for power delivery. The feature's 'solar panel' serves as the energy source, conceptually similar to the 'solar charging layer' as a solar energy collection and transfer component. Both also involve a 'charging' or power transfer function.
key_distinctions: The claim element specifies a 'solar charging layer' as the component responsible for charging, whereas the feature describes 'Charge Controller Circuitry.' While related to solar power, 'Charge Controller Circuitry' specifically denotes an active electronic circuit for regulating flow, which may or may not be synonymous with or fully encompassed by 'solar charging layer' in the patent context.

The ultimate target of the charging function is explicitly different: the feature charges an 'integrated battery pack' and provides power to a 'USB-C output,' whereas the claim element is configured to charge a 'light source.' These are functionally distinct power recipients.

The feature explicitly includes the functionality of 'preventing overcharge or deep discharge,' which describes specific power regulation and protection capabilities. The claim element's 'configured to charge' is a broader functional description that does not inherently specify these particular protective regulation mechanisms.
comparison_matrix.cannot_determine: Without reviewing the full patent specification and prosecution history, it is not possible to determine the precise claim construction of 'solar charging layer' or 'light source' as defined by the patentee. This automated comparison cannot assess whether the 'Charge Controller Circuitry' of the feature would be considered structurally or functionally equivalent to the 'solar charging layer' under the doctrine of equivalents, nor if 'charging a light source' could encompass charging a battery pack or powering a USB-C output within the context of the patent's scope.

comparison_matrix.feature_description: A collection of photovoltaic cells arranged to convert solar radiation into direct current electrical energy.
comparison_matrix.feature_patent_language: A plurality of photovoltaic cells configured to convert solar radiation into direct current electrical energy.
comparison_matrix.element_text: the solar charging layer electrically connects the light source and is configured to charge the light source
comparison_matrix.gemini_explanation: This claim element requires a component identified as a 'solar charging layer'. This layer must perform two specific functions: first, it must establish an electrical connection with a 'light source', and second, it must be configured to supply electrical energy to the light source for the purpose of 'charging' it. The term 'layer' typically implies a relatively thin or integrated component.
comparison_matrix.gemini_assessment: The user's 'Photovoltaic Panel Array' generates direct current electrical energy from solar radiation, which is functionally consistent with a 'solar charging layer' in its ability to produce power. However, the feature description does not explicitly state that this array 'electrically connects the light source' or is 'configured to charge the light source', which are specific functional requirements of the claim element.
key_distinctions: The feature describes an 'Array' of photovoltaic cells, which typically refers to a discrete, potentially macroscopic structure. The claim element specifies a 'layer,' which could imply a thinner, more integrated, or form-factor-specific component (e.g., thin-film, printed) that may differ structurally from a traditional panel array.

The feature broadly states the function of 'convert[ing] solar radiation into direct current electrical energy'. The claim element, conversely, mandates a specific functional connection and purpose: 'electrically connects the light source and is configured to charge the light source'. The feature does not explicitly detail this specific charging interaction with a light source.

The feature is a standalone description of an energy conversion device, while the claim element describes a component's role within a larger system, specifically its interface with and effect on 'the light source'.
comparison_matrix.cannot_determine: A human expert would need to perform claim construction for 'solar charging layer' and 'light source' within the context of the entire patent specification to ascertain if the structural and functional scope of 'Photovoltaic Panel Array' falls within the claimed 'layer' and its specific charging interaction. An analysis under the doctrine of equivalents might also be necessary if literal identity is absent.

The claim element specifies a 'solar charging layer', which may denote a specific physical form factor, integration method, or material characteristic (e.g., thinness, flexibility) that is not necessarily inherent in a generic 'panel array'.

The feature's stated function is solely 'to convert solar radiation into direct current electrical energy'. The claim element, however, imposes a highly specific functional context: the layer must specifically 'electrically connect the light source' and be 'configured to charge the light source'. This particular application is not mentioned in the feature.

The feature describes the primary function of a PV array itself. The claim element defines a role and connection within a broader system, specifically detailing an interaction with an external 'light source' for charging purposes.
comparison_matrix.cannot_determine: A human expert would need to interpret 'solar charging layer' based on the patent's specification to understand its full technical scope and whether a conventional 'Photovoltaic Panel Array' is encompassed. Furthermore, the meaning of 'light source' and 'charging' must be construed in the context of the claims and specification, and a doctrine of equivalents analysis might be required.

comparison_matrix.feature_description: A rechargeable battery pack directly incorporated within the device for accumulating and retaining electrical energy generated by the solar panel.
comparison_matrix.feature_patent_language: A rechargeable battery pack incorporated within the device, configured to accumulate and retain electrical energy generated by the solar panel.
comparison_matrix.element_text: the solar charging layer electrically connects the light source and is configured to charge the light source
comparison_matrix.gemini_explanation: This claim element requires a component identified as a 'solar charging layer' that is electrically connected to a 'light source'. The 'solar charging layer' must be specifically configured, designed, or adapted to transfer electrical energy to the 'light source' for the purpose of recharging or providing power to it, implying the light source either contains a rechargeable energy reservoir or operates directly from this supplied charge.
comparison_matrix.gemini_assessment: The feature describes a 'rechargeable battery pack' serving as an 'Integrated Energy Storage Unit' that accumulates and retains electrical energy generated by a solar panel. The claim element describes a 'solar charging layer' configured to charge a 'light source'. While the feature's battery pack would likely be part of an overall system involving solar charging, and a 'light source' that is charged often incorporates a battery, the feature explicitly describes the *storage component* (battery pack) and its function of accumulation. In contrast, the claim element describes the *charging component* (solar charging layer) and its function of charging a *light source*. There is no explicit recitation of a battery pack within the claim element's 'light source' or as the direct object being charged by the 'solar charging layer'.
key_distinctions: The feature explicitly recites a 'rechargeable battery pack' as an energy storage component, whereas the claim element recites a 'solar charging layer' as an energy transfer/generation component. These are distinct technical components with different primary functions (storage vs. charging/supply).

The feature's battery pack accumulates energy from a 'solar panel', whereas the claim element's 'solar charging layer' specifically connects to and is configured to charge a 'light source'. The claim does not explicitly mention an intermediate battery pack being charged by the 'solar charging layer', nor does it state that the 'light source' necessarily contains a battery pack.

The scope of the feature is focused on the battery's role in storing energy generated by a solar panel, while the claim element is focused on the 'solar charging layer's' connection and charging function relative to a 'light source'. The 'light source' is not necessarily synonymous with or limited to a 'rechargeable battery pack'.
comparison_matrix.cannot_determine: A human expert would need to perform a detailed claim construction for 'solar charging layer' and 'light source' based on the patent's specification to ascertain if the 'light source' is inherently understood to include or be a rechargeable battery pack, or if the 'solar charging layer' is understood to charge an intermediate battery. Analysis under the doctrine of equivalents might also be required to determine if the described 'rechargeable battery pack' serves the same function, in the same way, to achieve the same result as a component being charged by the 'solar charging layer', even if not literally recited.

comparison_matrix.feature_patent_language: A mechanical assembly configured to enable a photovoltaic panel array to articulate into a reduced volume configuration for portability.
comparison_matrix.element_text:
:
 a panel unit in which a plurality of light adjustment panels are stacked in a first direction, each light adjustment panel including a first substrate and a second substrate, the first substrate including a first terminal, the second substrate overlapping the first substrate and including a second terminal
comparison_matrix.gemini_explanation: This element describes a structural component, specifically a "panel unit." This unit comprises multiple "light adjustment panels" arranged in a "stacked" configuration along a "first direction." Each individual "light adjustment panel" is further defined as including a "first substrate" and a "second substrate," where the "second substrate" physically "overlaps" the "first substrate." Furthermore, the "first substrate" must incorporate a "first terminal," and the "second substrate" must incorporate a "second terminal." This structure suggests a layered, potentially electrically connected, panel designed to manipulate light.
comparison_matrix.gemini_assessment: The user's "Folding Panel Structure" describes a "mechanical assembly" designed for "photovoltaic panel array[s]" to physically "collapse or articulate into a reduced volume configuration for portability." The claim element, conversely, describes a "panel unit" composed of "light adjustment panels" that are "stacked in a first direction," with each panel having specific "first" and "second substrates" and "terminals." While both refer to multi-panel structures, the feature focuses on the *reconfigurability mechanism* and *photovoltaic function* for *portability*, whereas the claim element focuses on the *static, layered internal structure* and *light adjustment function* of its panels. There is no explicit mention in the claim element of a mechanism for collapsing or articulating the panel unit, nor does it specify a photovoltaic function.
key_distinctions: The feature explicitly defines a "photovoltaic panel array" for energy generation and focuses on "portability" via physical "collapse or articulation." The claim element specifies "light adjustment panels," implying a function related to controlling or modifying light, which is distinct from energy generation, and describes a static "stacked" arrangement rather than an articulating one.

The feature describes a "mechanical assembly" for active "collapse or articulation." The claim element describes a passive, static arrangement where panels are "stacked in a first direction," without specifying any mechanism for dynamic reconfiguration or reduction in volume.

The feature describes an overarching mechanical assembly enabling a *system-level* reconfigurability of an array. The claim element describes the *internal layered structure* and *static arrangement* of individual "light adjustment panels" within a "panel unit."
comparison_matrix.cannot_determine: Whether the term "light adjustment panels" could be construed to encompass or be equivalent to certain types of "photovoltaic panels" that may also incorporate light adjustment capabilities (e.g., smart windows, sun-tracking panels), requiring claim construction and expert testimony. Whether a "mechanical assembly enabling the photovoltaic panel array to collapse or articulate" would inherently include or be considered to implement a "panel unit" where "a plurality of light adjustment panels are stacked in a first direction," particularly regarding the *means* by which the stacking is achieved and if that "stacking" itself contributes to the collapse/articulation. This would require analysis under the doctrine of equivalents and potentially means-plus-function interpretation if the claim element were drafted in such a form.

comparison_matrix.feature_description: A housing designed to encapsulate and protect the folded solar panel and associated electronics, facilitating transport and providing durability.
comparison_matrix.feature_patent_language: A housing configured to encapsulate and protect a folded solar panel and associated electronics, thereby facilitating transport and providing durability.
comparison_matrix.element_text:
:
 a panel unit in which a plurality of light adjustment panels are stacked in a first direction, each light adjustment panel including a first substrate and a second substrate, the first substrate including a first terminal, the second substrate overlapping the first substrate and including a second terminal
comparison_matrix.gemini_explanation: This claim element defines a 'panel unit' as a structural assembly. It requires that a plurality of 'light adjustment panels' be stacked in a 'first direction' within this unit. Furthermore, each individual light adjustment panel must comprise a 'first substrate' containing a 'first terminal,' and a 'second substrate' that physically 'overlaps' the first substrate while also including a 'second terminal.' The element details the internal components and their specific arrangement.
comparison_matrix.gemini_assessment: The user feature describes a 'Compact Protective Enclosure,' which is explicitly defined as a housing designed to encapsulate and protect a folded solar panel and associated electronics, facilitating transport and providing durability. In contrast, the claim element from US-12449701-B2, Claim 1, describes a 'panel unit' composed of 'light adjustment panels' with specific internal substrate and terminal configurations. There is a fundamental technical distinction in the nature and primary function of these two entities. The feature is an outer, protective container, whereas the claim element is an internal, functional panel assembly with specific electrical and physical construction.
key_distinctions: The feature is an 'enclosure' or 'housing' which functions as an outer protective shell, while the claim element is a 'panel unit,' which is an internal, functional assembly of 'light adjustment panels.'

The primary function of the feature is 'encapsulation and protection' for transport and durability; the primary function of the claimed 'light adjustment panel unit' is implied to be light modulation or adjustment, based on its nomenclature and structural description.

The components described by the feature are a 'housing' for 'folded solar panel and associated electronics.' The claim element describes specific internal structural details: 'first substrate,' 'second substrate,' 'first terminal,' 'second terminal,' and their 'stacked' and 'overlapping' arrangement.

The scope differs significantly; the feature describes a high-level system component (the protective casing), while the claim element describes a specific, detailed sub-component (the panel unit) within a potentially larger system.
comparison_matrix.cannot_determine: Without specific claim construction of 'panel unit' and 'light adjustment panels' within the context of US-12449701-B2, it cannot be determined if such a 'panel unit' would constitute or be an integral part of a 'folded solar panel' or 'associated electronics' that the enclosure protects. An automated comparison cannot assess if the feature (an enclosure) could be considered equivalent to the claimed element (a specific panel unit) under the doctrine of equivalents, as their core structures and functions are distinct. Moreover, the claim element itself is not an enclosure.
